schedules.description: PAN, Aadhaar, address, bank account details, tax regime selection (old/new), nature of business/profession, GSTIN, whether tax audit applies.

keyFields.notes: Select industry code from CBDT list. Mandatory for business/profession income.
New regime is default. Old regime requires Form 10-IEA filed separately for business income filers.

keyFields.fieldName: Whether liable to tax audit u/s 44AB
keyFields.notes: Yes if turnover > ₹1Cr (business) or gross receipts > ₹50L (profession) or opted out of presumptive and income below minimum rate

commonMistakes: Selecting wrong business code
Not mentioning GSTIN when GST-registered
Not indicating partner status when income from firm

schedules.compulsoryCondition: If any salary income (including from firm as partner salary)
schedules.description: Salary from employer, allowances, perquisites, standard deduction. Also includes salary/remuneration received from partnership firm.

keyFields.notes: Total salary before deductions. From Form 16.
₹75,000 (new regime) or ₹50,000 (old regime)

commonMistakes: Not including perquisites (car, rent-free accommodation) in gross salary
Confusing partner remuneration (goes in BP) with salary from employer (goes in Schedule S)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule HP — Income from House Property
schedules.description: Rental income or loss from house properties. ITR-3 has no limit on number of properties (unlike ITR-1's 1 and ITR-4's 2 property limits).

keyFields.notes: Expected rent or actual rent — whichever is higher for let-out. Self-occupied = ₹0.
Deductible from GAV to arrive at Net Annual Value (NAV)
Auto-computed as 30% of NAV. Not available for self-occupied property.
Actual interest paid. Cap: ₹2L self-occupied (old regime only). Let-out: no cap. New regime: ₹0 for self-occupied.

commonMistakes: Claiming ₹2L interest deduction for self-occupied property under new regime (not allowed)
Not reporting deemed let-out income for second property (old regime)
Not carrying forward HP losses beyond ₹2L cap

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule BP — Income from Business or Profession
schedules.description: Complete business/profession income computation: gross receipts/turnover, gross profit, all business expenses, net profit, depreciation adjustments, disallowances u/s 37/40/40A, final business income. Separate sections for partner income from firm.

keyFields.notes: Total revenue before any deductions. Should match GST returns if GST registered.

keyFields.fieldName: Net profit as per P&L account
keyFields.notes: Bottom line from profit & loss statement before tax adjustments
Book depreciation — to be added back. IT Act depreciation claimed separately via Schedule DPM/DOA.
Cash payments > ₹10,000 (u/s 40A(3)), TDS defaults (u/s 40(a)(ia)), related party excess payments
If taxpayer is a partner — salary/commission/bonus received from firm. Taxable as business income.
Interest received from partnership firm on capital contribution. Taxable as business income.

commonMistakes: Not reconciling BP turnover with GSTR-1
Forgetting to add back book depreciation before claiming IT Act depreciation
Not reporting interest on firm capital as business income
Claiming personal expenses as business expenses

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule P&L — Profit and Loss Account
schedules.compulsoryCondition: If business/profession income exists and tax audit applies OR voluntarily provided
schedules.description: Condensed P&L account showing trading account (sales/purchases/gross profit) and P&L account (income/expenditure/net profit). Mandatory for tax audit cases. For non-audit, summary figures in Schedule BP are sufficient.

keyFields.notes: Stock at beginning of year
Net purchases (return inward deducted)
Department scrutinizes significant changes from prior year. GP ratio consistency across years reduces scrutiny risk.

commonMistakes: Not matching P&L figures with Balance Sheet figures
Failing to reconcile closing stock with physical stock

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Mandatory for tax audit cases; voluntary for others maintaining books
schedules.description: Condensed balance sheet showing proprietor's capital, secured/unsecured loans, fixed assets, current assets, debtors, bank/cash balance.

keyFields.notes: Opening capital ± profit/loss ± drawings
Outstanding receivables. Large debtors relative to revenue may trigger scrutiny.
Closing cash balance. High cash balance may require explanation.
Aggregate balance across all bank accounts as on March 31, 2026.

commonMistakes: Balance sheet not balancing (assets ≠ liabilities + capital)
Not including all bank accounts

schedules.compulsoryCondition: If any capital gains or capital losses in FY 2025-26
schedules.description: Capital gains from all asset types: LTCG/STCG on equity shares, equity MFs (Schedule 112A, 111A), property, unlisted shares, debt MF, gold, etc. All capital gain types supported in ITR-3 (unlike ITR-2, no restrictions).

keyFields.notes: Taxed at 12.5% above ₹1.25L. Grandfathering applies for pre-Jan 31, 2018 holdings.
Taxed at 20% (Finance Act 2024 change from 15%)
12.5% without indexation (Finance Act 2024). For property acquired pre-July 23, 2024: option to use 20% with indexation.
Unabsorbed capital losses can be carried forward in ITR-3 (unlike ITR-4 where CFL disqualifies). Fill Schedule CFL.

commonMistakes: Not applying ₹1.25L exemption before computing LTCG 112A tax
Using indexation for property acquired after July 23, 2024
Not reporting capital gains on debt mutual funds as short-term (no indexation from FY 2023-24)

schedules.compulsoryCondition: If interest, dividends, gaming winnings, or other non-classified income
schedules.description: Bank FD interest, savings interest, dividends, NSC interest, family pension, casual income, online gaming winnings (30% flat), VDA income.

keyFields.notes: Eligible for 80TTA deduction (₹10K, old regime) or 80TTB (₹50K, senior citizens)
Fully taxable at slab rate. No DTC exemption from FY 2020-21.
30% flat + 1% TDS u/s 194S. Transfer losses cannot offset other income.

commonMistakes: Not reporting FD interest accrued (even if not received)
Netting crypto profits and losses across different VDAs incorrectly

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule DPM — Depreciation on Plant & Machinery
schedules.compulsoryCondition: If business assets (plant, machinery, computers, vehicles) owned
schedules.description: Block-wise depreciation computation as per Income Tax Act rates. Separate from book depreciation. WDV opening balance, additions, deletions, rate, WDV closing balance.

keyFields.notes: Written-down value at start of year. Should match prior year closing WDV.
Cost of new assets added. Assets added after Oct 3: 50% depreciation for the year.
15% general P&M, 40% computers, 30% motor vehicles, 60% solar equipment. Applies to block WDV.

commonMistakes: Using straight-line (SLM) rates instead of WDV rates for IT Act purposes
Not claiming 50% depreciation for additions made in second half of year

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule DOA — Depreciation on Other Assets
schedules.compulsoryCondition: If buildings or other fixed assets (non-P&M) owned by business
schedules.description: Block-wise depreciation for buildings (10%, 5% for residential), intangibles (25%), furniture (10%).

keyFields.notes: 10% on WDV for commercial buildings; 5% for residential used in business; 40% for wooden/temporary structures

commonMistakes: Applying commercial rate (10%) to residential buildings
Not including land value separately (land is not depreciated)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule CFL — Carry Forward of Losses
schedules.compulsoryCondition: If any losses being carried forward from current or prior years
schedules.description: Year-wise carry-forward of: capital losses (short-term/long-term), house property losses, business losses (non-speculation), speculation losses, unabsorbed depreciation.

keyFields.notes: Can only offset LTCG in future years. Carry-forward for 8 years.
Can offset both STCG and LTCG in future. Carry-forward for 8 years.
Non-speculation business loss. Carry-forward for 8 years. Can offset any business income.

commonMistakes: Claiming CFL without having filed ITR in the loss year (loss cannot be carried forward if return not filed on time)
Offsetting long-term capital loss against short-term capital gains (not allowed)

schedules.compulsoryCondition: If claiming deductions u/s 10AA, 80HH to 80RRB, and adjusted total income > ₹20L
schedules.description: AMT at 18.5% of adjusted total income for individuals/HUFs claiming profit-linked deductions (SEZ units, special industry deductions). Not applicable to small taxpayers.

keyFields.notes: Regular income + amounts added back (10AA deductions, 80HH-80RRB deductions)
Tax if AMT > normal tax. Excess becomes AMTC credit u/s 115JD.

commonMistakes: Not computing AMT when claiming 10AA (SEZ) deduction
Forgetting AMT credit (AMTC) from prior years when normal tax exceeds AMT in later years

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Mandatory if any foreign bank account, investment, business interest, or immovable property held at any time during FY 2025-26
schedules.description: Comprehensive disclosure of all foreign assets. Residents (including RNOR and NRI returning to India) must disclose. Non-reporting attracts severe penalties under Black Money Act.

keyFields.notes: Account number, bank name, country, peak balance, closing balance. All foreign bank accounts including dormant ones.
Shares, bonds, debentures, other financial interests in foreign entities.

commonMistakes: Not disclosing RSUs (Restricted Stock Units) vested in foreign company employer
Forgetting NRE account held before returning to India (now taxable if resident)
Not disclosing foreign property even if inherited

schedules.compulsoryCondition: If any deductions claimed under Chapter VI-A (80C, 80D, 80G, etc.)
schedules.description: All personal deductions. Old regime only except 80CCD(2) and 80CCH. Business income filers choosing old regime must file Form 10-IEA. New regime allows only 80CCD(2) and 80CCH.

keyFields.fieldName: 80C deductions (LIC, PPF, ELSS, home loan principal)
keyFields.notes: Max ₹1.5L combined 80C+80CCC+80CCD(1). Old regime only.
14% of salary cap (raised from 10% in FY 2025-26 for private employees). Available in both regimes.
₹25K self + ₹25K parents (₹50K each if senior citizen). Old regime only.

commonMistakes: Claiming 80C under new regime (not allowed except 80CCD2, 80CCH)
Not filing Form 10-IEA when opting old regime with business income
Claiming 80TTA and 80TTB both (mutually exclusive)

schedules.description: Aggregation of all income heads: salary + HP + PGBP (business/profession) + capital gains + other sources. Sum equals Gross Total Income before deductions.
commonMistakes: HP loss set-off against other income limited to ₹2L
CG losses cannot offset PGBP income

schedules.description: Final computation: total income → tax at slab/special rates → surcharge → 4% cess → 87A rebate → TDS credit → advance tax → net payable or refund.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly computing surcharge (10% if income 50L-1Cr, 15% if >1Cr for individuals)
Not applying 87A rebate (₹60K for new regime if income ≤ ₹12L; ₹12.5K old regime if income ≤ ₹5L)
Not accounting for AMTC credit from prior years
